author: @MechanicForGMCCars
text:
Any help here please .
I got stuck with odd behaviore.
I have British stile dashboard suposedly it was build for me Ascona C 85 model year that thing has 700Y voltage regulator inside the dashboard.
How ever I broke down the 12si delco alternator regulator to see whats inside and suprisengly nothing just empty circuet board.
The thing is Im chaseing fir days to see why me engin self acelerates from idel to max rpm when I use me head lights the other thing acured as well If I use on warm motor all 4 blinkers me engin dont want to stop it kinde of works in union with blinker relay nearly resembeling hit and miss engine .
I do use 4 pin gm ignition module with inductive trigger and E core type ignition coil from GM .
I found the problem eventualy it was corroded ground wire on the back side lights bulb holder it hade grean staff on it I cleaned it and the problem gone away eventualy.
But I wanted that feature for some reason I was curiuse why it worked so well???
Can I modify that some how and use that feature ?
Other specs that I have:
2pin warning lights on relay,
3 pin blinker relay,
4pin rear windscrean heater relay 
6pin windscrean wiper motor relay.
So any help please why that acured?
What has blinker relay to do with other relays me wire harnest from GM is a mess Im confused like hell.
Is there any way to control that 4 pin gm ignition module pulse duration ? Im tired to go out side in winter to fumel with me distributor ignition timing .
Im seriusly anoyed.
And is that windscrean motor 6pin relay actualy singel pole dobel trow relay??

author: @chriswright9819
text:
Great video helped a lot as I have to sort out parking on my P5B
However just out of interest:Resistance is defined as the property of the conductor which opposes the flow of electric current. It is also defined as the ratio of the voltage applied to the electric current flowing through it. The resistance of a conductor depends on the length, area of cross-section, and the nature of the material that is used in the manufacturing of the conductor. For a conductor, the resistance is directly proportional to the length of the conductor and inversely proportional to the area of cross-section.

Resistivity is defined as the resistance offered by the material per unit length for unit cross-section. The SI unit of resistivity is Ohm.meter. Resistivity increases linearly with temperature. The resistivity of conductors is low when compared to the resistivity of the insulators. Therefore, it can be represented as:
Resistivity of conductors < Resistivity of alloys < Resistivity of insulators.

author: @haloweenuk2822
text:
Hi, is there any chance you might be able to help me with a wiper motor issue?
Whilst I use them I certainly don't fully understand the wiring system.
I use lots of wiper motors for my halloween displays (connected to12v 5am power packs/leads).
But whilst I can simply select fast or slow speed I am desperately trying to find the right combination for park mode on my 4 wire  Mapco 90192 wiper motor.
I have spent months trying to figure it out but I fail every time.
Any advice or a walk through would be greatly appreciated.
Best wishes 👍

author: @NorthernKitty
text: Given the speed at which they rotate under a 12V load, these motors would seem to be a good candidate for making a hand crank generator.  😊  Or are they horribly difficult to turn?

replies.author: @jessewoody5772
replies.text: Northernkitty.  Most are a worm gear drive and therefore cannot be back driven. A worm gear can turn a worm wheel but a worm wheel cannot turn a worm gear.
